title: potentilla pensylvanica var. longepilosa
intro: Potentilla pensylvanica var. longepilosa, also known as the long-haired cinquefoil, is a perennial herb that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The long-haired cinquefoil has been shown to exhibit various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting digestive health. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from minor skin irritations to more serious conditions like infections and digestive issues.
